calendar.matters:
Petition To Modify Restrictions In Gift Instrument
Off Calendar Sep-01-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Karl Mill appearing by CourtCall for California Masonic Foundation.

The Court stated that before it today is a Petition to Modify Restrictions in Gift Instrument, that the Attorney General (AG) was served with the petition, and that the AG filed a a statement of non-opposition to the petition. The Court also stated that at the last calling of the matter Judge Peter Busch wanted counsel to reform the proposed order to track the trust instrument and that it was now inquiring why counsel did not track the trust instrument verbatim as to a "medical career". Mr. Mill stated that the original language had the restriction of a college education and "medical", that the approach taken was to pay for college and follow with "medical education", that he and the prior judge spent a long time talking about it, and that he did his best to track what the prior judge wanted. Mr. Mill also stated that there is a broad reference to medical, that he feels that the prior judge was okay with it, that he is open to concerns and/or adjustments, referenced it being for undergraduate education/education for needy children, and stated that the AG is okay with the language. The Court and counsel confer regarding the original trust language, etc. Mr. Mill referenced page 4 of the Trust at paragraphs 4 & 5 noting that there is an explanation in the petition regarding grouping those paragraphs together. The Court addressed the scholarships, referenced the Masonic Homes and stated that it understands that there is a broad definition. Discussions are held regarding the proposed language as to "preference of medical". Mr. Mill noted that there is a restriction regarding undergraduate and graduate education. At the conclusion of discussions, the Court stated that it would take out the "with a preference" language and noted that it would interlineate on the proposed order. The Court then took the Petition to Modify Restrictions in Gift Instrument under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Matter not reported.

Petition To Modify Restrictions In Gift Instrument
Off Calendar Jul-21-2020 Continued To Aug-11-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Modify Restrictions In Gift Instrument
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jul-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Modify Restrictions In Gift Instrument
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Apr-28-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Modify Restrictions In Gift Instrument
Off Calendar Feb-26-2020 Continued To Mar-25-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Karl Mill appeared by telephone California Masonic Foundation.

The Court stated that it has no problem with doing modification to the gift instrument, but questions why the language should not be limited to medical students, read trust language regarding the scholarship, spoke of the Trustor's intent and stated that it does not see why certain language should be eliminated, or a basis to remove that goal. Discussion was held regarding an appropriate form of language to address the Court's concerns. The Petition to Modify Restrictions in Gift Instrument is continued to March 25, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Mill is to file a supplement and provide a new revised order by March 18, 2020.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.
